transcript: We have a cat. Okay, that's a lie. We have many cats. And let me tell you, there's nothing that these cats love more than food. This got us wondering, how would our cats react to three gourmet recipes made just for them? And which dish would they like the best? We're going to start out by making our super spoiled cats some gourmet gravy. And I do want to note that while we were prepping this, those cats were very interested. Let's get started. This gravy recipe comes from the SPCA website and I did you a favor and spared you having to see all the gross raw organ meats and precooked it. That goes in there. Gonna add in some stock, some flour, and our friend salt bee is gonna help too. Salt in high quantities is good for cats, but a little bit is alright. While this is thickening up, let me tell you about our cats. So first of all, we have Dally. And Dally is the bully. Next we have Rothko, and Rothko, he's the loud one. We also have Joaquin. Joaquin is the snuggly one. Then we have Summer, his sister, and she is the weirdest. Last but not least, we have Mimi. Mimi is the scaredy cat. Okay, this is looking like it's getting pretty thick. It smells great. It really doesn't. I think that's pretty much done. I'm kidding. What did you expect? It's cat food. It doesn't look good. So now that the room has the delightful fragrance of a cat food factory, we're gonna move on. I really kinda regret making that gravy first though. So we're gonna make just a piece of regular baked salmon, just to see if it's worth doing all this work for the spoiled cats or if they'd rather just have something easy. Just gonna put in a little bit of sunflower oil. Oh, there's a cat hair. That's appropriate. Sunflower oil. Just a little bit. In we go. And we're just gonna bake that till it's nice and flaky. Holy mackerel. These cats are gonna be excited. Okay, I gotta be honest. I've never opened a can of mackerel before in my life, and I have no idea what to expect. Ooh. Oof. Okay. Well, it smells like stuff my cats would like. So this recipe is essentially a pate with mackerel as the protein. This is our mackerel, some chicken broth, and a bit of sunflower oil. And we're going to pulse that until it all comes together. Honestly, mackerel just smells a lot like tuna. That's part two done. Final recipe is from lovet from love to know pets calm and this is a little bit of a healthier one some chicken pre-cooked steamed broccoli and carrots and some chicken broth let's make a pate so if I had to eat one of these I think this would be the one because it smells the best I'll do do it for science. Wow you are a brave man. Trust me I've had worse. That's true. Not bad. It's not great though. You're ridiculous. So now that we're all done and the quality has been tested we'll move on to the plating. We're almost ready for the taste test. We have here our three beautifully plated courses. And I will say that to even the playing field field I included a little bit of the gravy on each one because these cats do get gravy based cat food for dinner most nights. Let's bring in the cats! What's the cat food in the bin? Are you kidding me? Okay, we have a bin of cat food in here, and he wants that. He doesn't even want the food I just made. I think he's extra suspicious, because they're not normally allowed in this room. Nope. Yeah, he likes it. For the mackerel first. Definitely the gravy though. I do find it interesting that they don't go back and forth. They just finish the one they started with. Oh. What's wrong, sweetie? Well, he made his choice in the weirdest way. I like how he's still on the chair, eating like a proper gentleman. Would you like them closer? So much for a gentleman. You made off like a baguette. Well, I think we have his second choice. You know, I think he thinks we're messing with him. Meow. Remember when I told you that Mimi is a scaredy cat? Well, this happened. We decided to feed her in her usual spot, which went much better. And in the end, she chose the mackerel. So that was a pretty fun experiment, and I think the cats enjoyed it. The mackerel was the winner in terms of what the cats went for, but I think the true winner is the gravy because that was their favorite. So if you're gonna make one of the things, make the gravy. Hopefully you guys enjoyed this video. Please let us know in the comments if you did, and you can check the links in the description if you want to make these recipes at home. Talk to you later! Bye!
